# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.152.0/http/server.ts";
import { openKv } from 'https://deno.land/x/kv@v1.0.1/mod.ts';

const kv = await openKv(); // Initialize the KV store

async function handleRequest(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  
  if (url.pathname === "/search") {
    const userId = url.searchParams.get("userId");
    const filterParam = url.searchParams.get("filterParam");
    
    // Construct the key dynamically from user input
    const kvKey: any[] = ['users', userId, filterParam]; 
  
    let value;
    try {
      value = await kv.get(kvKey); // Fetch the data using constructed key
    } catch (err) {
      return new Response("Error fetching data", { status: 500 });
    }
    
    if (!value) {
      return new Response("No data found for provided query parameters", { status: 404 });
    }
  
    // Return the value as response to the request
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ key: kvKey, value: value }), { headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json' }});
  } else {
    return new Response("Not found", { status: 404 });
  }
}

serve(handleRequest); // Start the server on port 8000
